summary: Feature request to expose the private GRContext field as a public property on the Blazor SKGLView component, matching the pattern used by MAUI and macOS SKGLView implementations.

stepsToReproduce: Attempt to access GRContext on the view instance
Observe that the context field is private and inaccessible

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The Blazor SKGLView still has GRContext as a private field in the current codebase. No PR has been submitted to expose it.

analysis.summary: The Blazor SKGLView component stores its GRContext as a private field (line 24 of SKGLView.razor.cs), unlike the MAUI SKGLView which exposes it as a public property. The reporter needs direct access to the GRContext to create additional rendering surfaces. A workaround exists via SKSurface.Context (available through the OnPaintSurface event args), but this only works inside the paint callback and returns GRRecordingContext rather than GRContext.
analysis.rationale: This is an enhancement, not a feature request — the GRContext already exists as a private field in the Blazor view, it just needs to be exposed publicly. The MAUI and macOS views already expose this property, establishing the pattern. The fix is trivial (add a public property), and has 2 thumbs-up reactions indicating community interest.

keySignals.text: on macOS we can directly access the GRContext of the view
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter is comparing platform views and expects API parity.

keySignals.text: In the Blazor view this is not an option since the field is private
keySignals.interpretation: Correctly identified that GRContext is private in SKGLView.razor.cs line 24.

keySignals.text: i'm using the GRContext of the view to generate an additional surface for rendering
keySignals.interpretation: Concrete use case: creating secondary GPU-backed surfaces from the shared GL context.

codeInvestigation.finding: GRContext is declared as 'private GRContext? context;' — not accessible outside the component
MAUI exposes 'public GRContext? GRContext => lastGRContext;' — establishes the pattern this request follows
ISKGLView interface defines 'GRContext? GRContext { get; }' as part of the contract
SKSurface.Context property returns GRRecordingContext — partial workaround via event args Surface, but returns base type not GRContext

workarounds: Access GRContext via e.Surface.Context inside the OnPaintSurface callback (returns GRRecordingContext, cast to GRContext if needed)
nextQuestions: Should the Blazor SKGLView implement ISKGLView for consistency with MAUI?
Should the GRContext be accessible outside the paint callback, or only within it?

resolution.hypothesis: The Blazor SKGLView was implemented without exposing GRContext as a public property, unlike other platform views that follow a consistent pattern.

proposals.title: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
proposals.description: Add a public read-only property that exposes the private context field, matching the MAUI pattern: 'public GRContext? GRContext => context;'

proposals.title: Workaround: access GRContext via Surface.Context
proposals.description: Inside the OnPaintSurface callback, cast e.Surface.Context to GRContext to obtain the GL context for creating additional surfaces.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public GRContext property to Blazor SKGLView
resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial one-line change that aligns Blazor with MAUI and macOS views. The workaround is limited to the paint callback scope.

actionability.reason: Valid enhancement with clear implementation path and established pattern from other platform views. Trivial to implement but needs maintainer decision on whether to align Blazor view API with MAUI/macOS.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge request with workaround and implementation guidance
